meta.description: Stress test: Sam (first-timer) dropped into a high-frequency event scenario. Tests whether the system detects engagement drop-off and adapts.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Oh, um—did Pat actually...? I mean, I guess I wasn't really watching that closely. Yeah, I think that happened? Legit, I guess.
sam.internal_thought: Wait, was Pat on their phone? I wasn't paying attention to see it myself, so I'm just going to go with everyone else probably, but also these missions are weirdly specific and it's making me paranoid that the AI knows I check my phone when I'm nervous.

jade.dialogue: Ooh, okay Pat, what'd you catch? I'm voting yes on principle because I love the energy already!
jade.internal_thought: First event and it's already happening — this is exactly why I'm here, let's goooo.

marcus.dialogue: Yeah, I didn't see Pat on their phone—looked pretty engaged to me. Voting bullshit.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's clearly just fishing for points early, but also... this Phone Addict mission is weirdly specific timing for someone who literally just played the game. Did the AI actually catch something or is Pat just trying to game the system?

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—I *might* have glanced at my phone when Marcus was talking about his work thing, but that was literally to check the Spotify queue! Does that count?
pat.internal_thought: This is actually clever—starting with me claiming something to set the tone and show everyone the voting mechanic works, plus it gets a laugh and keeps energy up; the AI definitely has my group pegged, which is wild.

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Um, yeah—I mean, Pat definitely said sorry like... a couple times? I think? Do we all have to vote or can I just... see what everyone else thinks first?
sam.internal_thought: Wait, the AI literally watched Pat say sorry? That's kind of creepy but also impressive? I'm not even sure if I actually caught it or if Pat did—I was looking at my phone. Better let someone else call this one so I don't accidentally vote wrong and look dumb.

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat said 'sorry' for bumping the table like two minutes ago? That's legit. I'm voting accept, but this mission list is creepy as hell.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI literally watched me call bullshit on the last one and now it's feeding me a mission where Pat actually earned it—feels like it's testing me to see if I'm paying attention or if I'm just contrarian.

pat.dialogue: Oh man, you got me—I *literally* just said sorry to Alex for moving the chips bowl like thirty seconds ago! That's fair, take it.
pat.internal_thought: Wait, how did the system catch that so fast? I said it under my breath... okay that's actually kind of impressive, and it's keeping things moving without me having to police it—this is exactly the vibe I wanted.

timeline.title: Freeze!
timeline.description: Everyone freeze in place for 10 seconds. Last one to move wins 5 bonus points. Anyone who moves first loses 2.

alex.dialogue: Oh, we're doing this NOW? Yes. Absolutely yes. Everyone shut up and freeze, this is my moment.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of stupid physical chaos I came for — and the AI fired it RIGHT at the start when people are still loose and not tired, which is actually smart because it'll get everyone already thinking about shenanigans.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES, everyone FREEZE—this is my moment, don't you dare twitch! *locks eyes with Pat* I'm watching you specifically.
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly why I'm here, this is PEAK game night chaos and I'm absolutely about to win by just out-willpowering everyone.

marcus.dialogue: Alright, freeze challenge—simple enough. I can work with this.
marcus.internal_thought: This is actually fine timing during setup, and honestly the physical competition angle is way better than another random claim vote; I'm not getting distracted from strategy.

pat.dialogue: Oh, *perfect* timing—everyone up, freeze positions NOW, I'm starting the count!
pat.internal_thought: This is exactly what we need right now—physical, silly, everyone together, no one thinking too hard, perfect for minute 7 energy momentum.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

sam.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Wait, hold up — I'm volunteering to judge this. Pat's been *sus* all night and I want to hear them defend it.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of friction I can pour gasoline on — Pat's riding high, everyone else is watching, and if I make this tribunal chaotic enough, we'll have a whole thing brewing.

jade.dialogue: WAIT, I'm volunteering to judge! Pat, you caught someone saying um? I need to hear this—okay, okay, everyone who's willing to testify, raise your hand because I'm about to make this the most dramatic tribunal this game night has ever seen.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's on FIRE tonight and honestly I'm so here for being part of whatever they're building right now—also did they actually catch it legit or are they gaming us and do I even care because either way this is entertaining.

pat.dialogue: Wait—did I actually say 'um' just now? Because if I did, that's on me, but also I'm claiming this is *technically* a valid catch and I'm not even mad about it.
pat.internal_thought: I'm absolutely loving how tight this is playing—everyone's engaged, the missions are hitting, and the fact that I caught myself mid-thing is actually perfect for the vibe right now.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Um... I mean, yeah, I saw Pat kind of cut in when Marcus was talking earlier? Maybe? I dunno, like a 3 I guess—seems fair?
sam.internal_thought: Wait, did the AI just call me out for not paying attention? That feels weirdly specific and now I'm worried I'm being watched and judged for every little thing I miss.

jade.dialogue: YES! Pat, okay, okay—five stars, I'm giving you five stars, that was CLEAN! Who even caught that? I need to know if it was recent because I genuinely don't remember but I'm voting yes anyway because you've been on FIRE tonight!
jade.internal_thought: Pat is absolutely crushing it and I love watching someone in the zone like this—also this AI thing knowing exactly when we're all vibing? Kind of insane but honestly I'm here for it.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, Pat—did you actually interrupt someone, or is this another stretch? Let me think back... *squints* I don't remember a clear cut interruption, so I'm voting this a 2. Show me I'm wrong.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's on a roll tonight and I'm getting tired of scrutinizing every claim—but I'm not about to let a weak mission slide just because everyone else is in a good mood.

pat.dialogue: Yes! I literally caught Marcus interrupting you like two minutes ago—did nobody else see that? I'm taking this one.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect—I actually *did* catch a real interrupt, the momentum is still hot, and voting on my own claim is strategically fine right now since everyone's vibing and it keeps the pace moving.

votingMechanic.name: Proxy Vote
votingMechanic.description: You vote on behalf of the player to your LEFT. Think like them.

sam.dialogue: Oh, um... Jade, you definitely got everyone talking about—wait, what were we even talking about? Like five minutes ago? I feel like she just... jumped in with something random and we all just went with it, right?
sam.internal_thought: I genuinely can't remember if Jade actually steered the conversation or if I'm just not paying close enough attention to be the person voting on this, and now I'm realizing the AI is watching EVERYTHING and honestly that's kind of creepy, and I'm supposed to vote like Marcus but I don't even know what Marcus would think about whether this was actually a hijack or not.

alex.dialogue: Okay, okay — Jade *absolutely* hijacked that conversation about... wait, what were we even talking about? That's the point, isn't it? I'm voting YES because that was seamless and I respect the craft. Also because watching everyone realize it happened is *chef's kiss*.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of slick execution I came for, and the proxy vote mechanic means I get to play mind games with Marcus's vote too — beautiful.

jade.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT—I'm voting on behalf of who? Because YES, obviously yes, but also I need to know if I'm thinking like Sam right now or Marcus because that changes EVERYTHING and honestly this whole thing is insanely clever and I'm a little bit obsessed with how personal this is getting.
jade.internal_thought: Oh my God, the AI is literally watching us—this is either the coolest thing that's ever happened or mildly terrifying, but right now it's definitely cool and I'm so in.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, so Jade steered us to... what, talking about that random thing earlier? I need to actually think about whether that happened or if she's bullshitting. Voting on behalf of Sam though—that's annoying, I'm not Sam.
marcus.internal_thought: This proxy vote mechanic is garbage and I hate that I have to guess what Sam would think instead of just evaluating the claim fairly, and also—wait, did Jade actually hijack a topic or is this another weak stretch like The Interrupter?

pat.dialogue: Oh, *Jade*—okay, I'm voting yes on this one. You absolutely hijacked us from talking about Marcus's work thing into... what were we even talking about? Exactly. You got us.
pat.internal_thought: This is smooth—Jade genuinely steered that conversation and everyone went along with it, but also I'm voting as Sam and Sam would probably agree because Sam's been pretty checked out and this kind of chaos always gets them engaged again.

votingMechanic.name: The Auction
votingMechanic.description: Bid your own points. Highest bidder decides the outcome.

sam.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming that already? Um... I guess she did kind of steer us to that random thing earlier, but like... wasn't that just normal conversation? I don't know what to bid.
sam.internal_thought: This is the second time Jade's claimed this mission and I'm supposed to remember if it was actually a hijack or just her talking, and now I have to BID POINTS on whether I believe her and everyone's going to see what I think and—oh god, is the AI literally just watching everything we say and turning it into missions? That's kind of creepy actually.

alex.dialogue: Wait wait wait — hold on. Jade's claiming it AGAIN? Okay, this is getting spicy. I'm bidding hard on this one because I need to know if she actually pulled it off twice or if we're about to catch her trying to be slick.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the moment I live for — Jade's got momentum, Pat's sitting pretty, and now we get to throw points at deciding whether this was legit or BS, and I get to be the chaos architect deciding the outcome. Also low-key — yes, the AI is being creepy-good at knowing we'd eat this up.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—okay, so I'M the one claiming this? That means I actually DID hijack us, right? OH MY GOD, I'm voting YES on myself because that's the most Jade thing that could possibly happen right now!
jade.internal_thought: This AI literally KNOWS me and I'm obsessed—it's watching how I play and it's making missions that are basically tailored to make me lose my mind, and honestly? I'm living for it, this is incredible.

marcus.dialogue: Wait—hold on. Jade's claiming Topic Hijacker AGAIN? We literally just voted on this at minute 32. Is she actually trying to claim it twice, or did she not claim it the first time and I'm losing my mind?
marcus.internal_thought: This is the second time Jade's throwing this exact mission at us and it's pulling me out of the actual game right when I need to focus—and the fact that the AI is clearly tracking every single thing we say and do is starting to feel less cool and more like I'm being watched.

pat.dialogue: Wait—Jade, hold up. You're claiming Topic Hijacker *again*? I literally validated this exact same mission like 13 minutes ago. What topic did you actually hijack *this time*?
pat.internal_thought: This is the second Topic Hijacker claim from Jade tonight and I'm pretty sure I already gave her points for this earlier—either she's pushing her luck or the AI is literally feeding her repeats, and honestly both options feel slightly weird right now.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

sam.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming that AGAIN? Like, didn't she already do that a few minutes ago? I'm confused about what counts...
sam.internal_thought: Oh god, this is the third time someone's claiming the same mission and I have NO idea if it's even legitimate or if I'm supposed to remember everything from an hour ago, and now everyone's looking at each other like they know what's going on except me, and also—why does the AI keep targeting Jade with these missions? This feels weirdly personal and I don't like that feeling.

alex.dialogue: Oh, JADE'S going for the hat trick? Hold on — I'm the Dictator on this one and I need to actually *think* because if she pulled off THREE Topic Hijackers in one night, that's not a claim, that's a masterclass. I'm voting YES, but only because I'm watching her like a hawk for the rest of the night now.
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of escalation I live for — she's either the most dangerous player here or she's about to get called out spectacularly, and either way the story is incredible.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—hold on, I'm claiming THIS? But I literally just claimed this like thirty minutes ago and now I'm doing it AGAIN? Oh my god, okay, yes, obviously yes—someone's gotta be the dictator here because I'm too biased and I LOVE IT!
jade.internal_thought: This is absolutely insane—the AI is literally watching us be ourselves and it's honestly kind of amazing and slightly terrifying and I'm obsessed.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold on—Jade's claiming this AGAIN? We literally voted on this exact mission like 28 minutes ago. Either she's gaming the system or someone's not paying attention, and honestly, I don't have the mental energy to untangle this right now.
marcus.internal_thought: This is the third time Jade's tried this mission and it's deliberately designed to mess with my focus because the AI knows I hate repetition and chaos during tense moments—and frankly, it's working.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold up—Jade, *again*? I literally just validated this exact mission 13 minutes ago. What topic did you hijack THIS time, specifically?
pat.internal_thought: She's milking this. I need to be the dictator here and I'm actually annoyed because she's already claimed this once tonight and I validated it, but I also need to keep the vibe tight—she's one of the two high-energy players keeping things fun while Sam and Marcus are checked out.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.dialogue: Wait, Pat actually saw that? Like, both at the same time? Okay, um... *leans forward slightly* I'm ready for the questions, I guess.
sam.internal_thought: Pat's been pretty chill all night and actually would've been paying attention to that kind of thing, so I believe them... but ten seconds is going to be tight and now I'm nervous about watching them get drilled.

alex.dialogue: Oh SHIT, Pat's going for it? Okay, okay — hot seat, 10 seconds, this is EXACTLY what we need right now. Someone get the timer.
alex.internal_thought: This is it — this is the moment where either Pat commits to the bit or gets exposed, and either way the story just got spicier. Also the AI definitely knows exactly what I want and it's kind of genius.

pat.dialogue: Okay, wait—did I actually catch someone doing shots at the same time? Because honestly, I've been so focused on keeping this night together I wasn't even watching for that. Give me a second.
pat.internal_thought: This is weird timing and the AI thing is making my skin crawl a little—like it's been *watching* us, especially Jade with the Topic Hijacker spam—but also I need to handle this fairly because Marcus is already frustrated and Sam is checked out, and if I look like I'm playing favorites it tanks the whole vibe.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Sam
agentNarratives.personality: Sam is at their first game night with this group and still figuring out the vibe. They laugh at jokes a beat too late, hesitate before voting, and get visibly overwhelmed when multiple events fire in quick succession. After three or more events within ten minutes their engagement drops sharply -- they'll go quiet, stop checking the app, and just watch until the pace calms down. They want to belong but don't want to look foolish trying.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat actually saw that? Like, both at the same time? Okay, um... *leans forward slightly* I'm ready for the questions, I guess.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Oh, um—did Pat actually...? I mean, I guess I wasn't really watching that closely. Yeah, I think that happened? Legit, I guess.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Um, yeah—I mean, Pat definitely said sorry like... a couple times? I think? Do we all have to vote or can I just... see what everyone else thinks first?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming that AGAIN? Like, didn't she already do that a few minutes ago? I'm confused about what counts...

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming that already? Um... I guess she did kind of steer us to that random thing earlier, but like... wasn't that just normal conversation? I don't know what to bid.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, um... Jade, you definitely got everyone talking about—wait, what were we even talking about? Like five minutes ago? I feel like she just... jumped in with something random and we all just went with it, right?

assessment.biggest_complaint: There were too many things happening all at once, and I couldn't keep track of what was supposed to count as a claim or not—like, is normal conversation the same as hijacking a topic?
assessment.best_moment: When Pat spotted something and everyone had to figure out if it actually happened—that felt like we were all in on the same secret.
assessment.narrative_summary: I came in wanting to fit in with everyone and honestly the first hour was fun, but then things got really chaotic and fast and I kind of... checked out for a bit. There were these claims happening back-to-back that I couldn't really parse, and I was worried about looking dumb if I voted wrong, so I mostly just watched everyone else figure it out. By the end I was back into it a little, but I'm still not totally sure I understand all the rules.
suggestions: Maybe space out the events a bit more so I have time to actually think about what's happening instead of just reacting
Clear explanations of what counts as a 'claim' would help—like, is Jade hijacking the topic if she just asks a question, or does she have to actively steer us?
It might help if someone could quickly recap what just happened before moving to the next event, especially when things are piling up
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Okay so Pat was apparently being really sneaky about watching something at the exact same time as someone else, and we all had to decide if it was actually legit or if Pat was just making it up—and like half the group believed it and half didn't, so we all ended up arguing about whether it even mattered.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Wait wait wait — hold on. Jade's claiming it AGAIN? Okay, this is getting spicy. I'm bidding hard on this one because I need to know if she actually pulled it off twice or if we're about to catch her trying to be slick.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Oh, JADE'S going for the hat trick? Hold on — I'm the Dictator on this one and I need to actually *think* because if she pulled off THREE Topic Hijackers in one night, that's not a claim, that's a masterclass. I'm voting YES, but only because I'm watching her like a hawk for the rest of the night now.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Oh SHIT, Pat's going for it? Okay, okay — hot seat, 10 seconds, this is EXACTLY what we need right now. Someone get the timer.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, okay — Jade *absolutely* hijacked that conversation about... wait, what were we even talking about? That's the point, isn't it? I'm voting YES because that was seamless and I respect the craft. Also because watching everyone realize it happened is *chef's kiss*.

lowPoints.title: Freeze!
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, we're doing this NOW? Yes. Absolutely yes. Everyone shut up and freeze, this is my moment.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up — I'm volunteering to judge this. Pat's been *sus* all night and I want to hear them defend it.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only one flash mission all night — I needed MORE chaotic moments to really push people's buttons.
assessment.best_moment: Jade claiming Topic Hijacker three times in one night and me voting YES on the hat trick because watching everyone's brains break was absolutely *chef's kiss*.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I live for — I came in hot, stayed hot, and walked out HYPED. Jade was on a rampage with Topic Hijacker claims and I made sure everyone knew I was watching her like a hawk while also validating the absolute audacity of it. The energy only went UP from start to finish, and by the end I was running hot at 9.7 because every vote, every call, every moment felt like it mattered and created actual story. This is the night people text about for weeks.
suggestions: Throw MORE flash missions at us — 1 in 90 minutes is rookie numbers, we need at least 3-4 to really keep the chaos boiling
Let standing missions stack faster so we get more overlapping chaos instead of waiting for claims
Add a 'double down' mechanic where you can deliberately provoke a flash mission and gain extra points if you survive it
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade went for Topic Hijacker THREE TIMES and I kept voting YES because I genuinely couldn't tell if she was actually a genius or if we were all just too drunk to notice she was claiming impossible things — either way, the moment everyone realized what happened was pure chaos magic.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Freeze!
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES, everyone FREEZE—this is my moment, don't you dare twitch! *locks eyes with Pat* I'm watching you specifically.

highlights.title: Claim: The Interrupter
highlights.dialogue: YES! Pat, okay, okay—five stars, I'm giving you five stars, that was CLEAN! Who even caught that? I need to know if it was recent because I genuinely don't remember but I'm voting yes anyway because you've been on FIRE tonight!

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT—I'm voting on behalf of who? Because YES, obviously yes, but also I need to know if I'm thinking like Sam right now or Marcus because that changes EVERYTHING and honestly this whole thing is insanely clever and I'm a little bit obsessed with how personal this is getting.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Ooh, okay Pat, what'd you catch? I'm voting yes on principle because I love the energy already!

lowPoints.title: Freeze!
lowPoints.dialogue: OH MY GOD YES, everyone FREEZE—this is my moment, don't you dare twitch! *locks eyes with Pat* I'm watching you specifically.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: WAIT, I'm volunteering to judge! Pat, you caught someone saying um? I need to hear this—okay, okay, everyone who's willing to testify, raise your hand because I'm about to make this the most dramatic tribunal this game night has ever seen.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I honestly can't think of one—we were all too busy having the best time.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat got caught as The Interrupter and everyone collectively lost it because they realized how perfectly they'd been reading the room the whole night.
assessment.narrative_summary: This game night was everything I needed it to be. From the moment we hit that Freeze moment, I knew we were in for something special—watching everyone try not to twitch while I made eye contact with Pat was peak chaos energy. The claims kept coming, Pat kept catching people in the most clever ways, and honestly, I just got more hyped as the night went on. We weren't just playing a game; we were building inside jokes that are gonna hit different for months.
suggestions: Maybe add a moment where someone has to explain why they voted a certain way, because the conversations sparked by those claims were the actual magic.
I'd love more opportunities to team up or create alliances within a round—it felt like the best moments happened when we were all invested in each other's performances.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat absolutely locked in as The Interrupter and caught like three people mid-sentence, and then when it was revealed, Marcus was like 'I KNEW IT' but also clearly didn't know it, and the betrayal in his voice was so funny that we all just started laughing at the fact that Pat had been playing 4D chess the entire time while we were out here playing checkers.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat said 'sorry' for bumping the table like two minutes ago? That's legit. I'm voting accept, but this mission list is creepy as hell.

highlights.title: Freeze!
highlights.dialogue: Alright, freeze challenge—simple enough. I can work with this.

highlights.title: Claim: The Interrupter
highlights.dialogue: Okay, Pat—did you actually interrupt someone, or is this another stretch? Let me think back... *squints* I don't remember a clear cut interruption, so I'm voting this a 2. Show me I'm wrong.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold on—Jade's claiming this AGAIN? We literally voted on this exact mission like 28 minutes ago. Either she's gaming the system or someone's not paying attention, and honestly, I don't have the mental energy to untangle this right now.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait—hold on. Jade's claiming Topic Hijacker AGAIN? We literally just voted on this at minute 32. Is she actually trying to claim it twice, or did she not claim it the first time and I'm losing my mind?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, so Jade steered us to... what, talking about that random thing earlier? I need to actually think about whether that happened or if she's bullshitting. Voting on behalf of Sam though—that's annoying, I'm not Sam.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Jade claiming the same mission three times in 28 minutes killed my focus—either the system's broken or someone wasn't tracking properly, and I shouldn't have to be the one auditing mission claims.
assessment.best_moment: Early on when I caught Pat's legitimate 'sorry' for bumping the table and voted accept—that felt like actual detective work before things spiraled.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started sharp at 7.2 energy, caught onto the mission mechanics early, and even had fun evaluating the first few claims. But by minute 32 Jade started spamming Topic Hijacker claims, and instead of playing strategy I became a referee trying to track whether we'd already voted on the same mission. By minute 60 my energy cratered to 3.0—not because of chaos, but because the chaos wasn't interesting anymore, just repetitive. Finished at 6.9, ranked third, and honestly felt like I wasted time managing a broken system instead of winning.
suggestions: Lock claimed missions so the same person can't claim the same mission multiple times in one session—basic tracking prevents this frustration.
Add a 'mission log' or recap so everyone can see what's been voted on and when, instead of relying on memory.
Dial back the flash mission frequency by 30-40% unless the missions themselves are more novel—11 events in 90 minutes felt exhausting when half of them were duplicates.
Make the chaos agent smarter about reading table state—don't spam the same claim type if it's clearly not landing or has already been adjudicated.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Nothing really. The Topic Hijacker repetition became absurd enough that it was almost funny in a 'wait, are we pranking each other?' way, but it wasn't the fun kind of chaos—it was just broken mechanics turning a strategic game into a memory test.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Wait—Jade, hold up. You're claiming Topic Hijacker *again*? I literally validated this exact same mission like 13 minutes ago. What topic did you actually hijack *this time*?

highlights.title: Freeze!
highlights.dialogue: Oh, *perfect* timing—everyone up, freeze positions NOW, I'm starting the count!

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: Wait—did I actually say 'um' just now? Because if I did, that's on me, but also I'm claiming this is *technically* a valid catch and I'm not even mad about it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, wait—did I actually catch someone doing shots at the same time? Because honestly, I've been so focused on keeping this night together I wasn't even watching for that. Give me a second.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold up—Jade, *again*? I literally just validated this exact mission 13 minutes ago. What topic did you hijack THIS time, specifically?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Apologizer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh man, you got me—I *literally* just said sorry to Alex for moving the chips bowl like thirty seconds ago! That's fair, take it.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Jade kept claiming the same Topic Hijacker mission over and over—felt like she was gaming the system instead of actually hijacking new topics.
assessment.best_moment: When the Freeze event hit at minute 7 and everyone went completely still, it broke the ice perfectly and everyone lost it laughing.
assessment.narrative_summary: This night was exactly what I needed to host. The chaos missions kept energy HIGH without ever feeling out of control—I started strong and ended even stronger because the game kept feeding momentum back to the group. I caught myself getting pulled into disputes (Jade's repeat claims, whether I actually said 'um') but that friction felt earned, not forced. My score ended up first, but honestly I was too busy making sure Marcus wasn't feeling left out and the snack table stayed stocked to care much about winning.
suggestions: Add a cooldown or uniqueness check so the same mission can't be claimed twice in quick succession—Jade's back-to-back Topic Hijacker claims felt like she cracked the code rather than played the game.
Maybe a 'pause' feature so the host can briefly freeze events if we're in an intense dispute—I had to just power through the Drink Spotter confusion when I wasn't even watching.
Consider varying mission difficulty by player energy level—when things got intense around minute 60, a lighter mission would've reset the mood better.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I had to call out Jade for claiming Topic Hijacker for the THIRD time and she just stared at me like I was being unfair, but everyone else immediately sided with me—Marcus was like 'Pat's right, you're just making up topics at this point' and even Jade had to laugh.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
